title: Cell-free extract,  4x Wizard mix and CFPS reaction preparation- Haseloff Lab
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4jara","text":"Following this recipe, you will cell-free extracts to perform Tx-TL reactions using plasmid or linear DNA based on P70a and T7 promoters. materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"140ui","text":"LB medium","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"o2m4","text":"2XTYG","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2vjta","text":"1M IPTG (optional)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8op0","text":"TBST Buffer, pH=7.6","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3g5u","text":"S30 buffer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5987f","text":"S30 dialysis buffer (optional)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2o46e","text":"2M DTT","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cs9pt","text":"1000mM Magnesium glutamate","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cgv6u","text":"10x Salt Solution mix","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1grab","text":"25x Nucleotide mix","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"92nn","text":"25x 19 Amino Acid Mix","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9adr","text":"25x PEP","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4cj2m","text":"40% PEG 8000","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5sn48","text":"Plasmid or linear DNA purified (20 mM)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 5 mL started culture ( BL21 DE Star ) were grown overnight at 37°C from a single colony in LB plate.
At the next day 50 mL of LB medium were inoculated with 500 µL of the stationary culture from step 1 and grown at overnight at 37°C.
At day 3, 12 mL of the stationary culture were inoculated into 400 mL of 2xYTG and grown at 37°C on an orbital shaker at 200 rpm in a 2.5 L baffled Tunair flask.

Culture were grown to the exponential phase optical density (OD600) 2.5-3.0 for approximately 4h. # If you are planning to use plasmid/ linear DNA based on T7 promoter, the culture needs to be induced with 400µL 1 M IPTG when it reaches an OD of 0.6.
Quickly, chill the cells on water/ice bath and incubate on ice for 15 min. #From now on everything has to be performed on ice.
Split the culture in two and centrifugate for 12 min at 5000g at 4°C to pellet the cell. # Use 250 mL centrifuge bottles.

Wash the 3 times the pellets with each time resuspending them in the buffer: two times with cold TBST and the last with cold S30 buffer supplemented with 2 mM dithiothreitol (DTT). Every time for 12 min at 5000g at 4°C.
Keep and flash frozen the pellet in a pre-weighted 50mL falcon tube. # The pellet can be stored at -80°C from 1 to 2 weeks.

Before opening the cells, weight the pellet and resuspend into cold S30 buffer supplemented with 2 mM DTT. #You have to know the weight as you do not want to overdilute the lysate. Rule of thumb is going for 1g of cells/0.9ml of buffer.

Open cell quickly with Emulsiflex ~17500-25000 psi and collect the lysate, overall suspension must be passed once. # Wait until pressures changes are stabilized. Alternatively, you can use another cell disruption system as sonication.

Alternatively, to lysate the cell suspension by sonication, use a QSonica Q125 sonicator with a 3.175 mm diameter probe at a frequency of 20 kHz and 50% amplitude by 10 s ON/OFF pulses for a total of 60 s (delivering∼350 J).
Transfer the lysate into chilled tubes and centrifuge for 30min at 30 000 g and 4°C, collect 2/3 of the supernatant and repeat the procedure.
Then, cell lysate was aliquoted, flash frozen and stored at -80°C.

A) Alternatively, a runoff reaction can be performed by transferring the lysate into a falcon tube and incubate at 37°C for 80 min at 200 rpm in dark conditions. Following this incubation, the extract was centrifugated for 10 min at 4°C and 12000g. Then, supernatant was aliquoted, flash frozen and stored at -80°C.

B) Alternatively, a runoff reaction can be performed by transferring the lysate into a falcon tube and incubate at 37°C for 80 min at 200 rpm in dark conditions. Following this incubation, the extract was centrifugated for 10 min at 4°C and 12000g. Then, supernatant was removed and injected into 10K MWCO and dialyzed against 600 mL of S30 Dialysis buffer. After, the extract was centrifugated for 10 min at 4°C and 12000g. Supernatant was aliquoted, flash frozen and stored at -80°C.
To prepare 500 µL of 4X Wizard mix , thaw the aliquots of each precursor solution and pipette the required volume following the order in the next table. %s %s

To set up a single cell-free reaction of 12µL, thaw the aliquots of each precursor solution and pipette the required volume following the order in the next table. # Cell-free extract must be added always at the end. A B 1 Precursor solution Required quantity (µL) 2 1)Autoclaved MQ water 1.4 3 2)40% PEG 8000 0.6 4 3)DNA (20 nM) 3 5 4)4x Wizard mix 3 6 5)Cell extract 4 7 Final Volume 12 #40% PEG 8000 should be stored at room temperature. Use microwave to dissolve the PEG 8000, , if it is necessary.
To prepare the all solutions described in this file, see the adequate version from protocol " S olutions for CFPS".

Deconstructing Cell-Free Extract Preparation for in Vitro Activation of Transcriptional Genetic Circuitry.. ACS synthetic biology. Rapid and Scalable Preparation of Bacterial Lysates for Cell-Free Gene Expression.. ACS synthetic biology. Simplifying and streamlining Escherichia coli-based cell-free protein synthesis.. Biotechnology progress.
V1-0 →V1-1 Protocol to cell disruption by sonication was added. V1-1→ DNA concentration in protocol " Performing cell-free reactions" was corrected
